title: Access to electricity, rural (% of rural population)
definition: Access to electricity, rural is the percentage of rural population with access to electricity.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: SDG 7.1.1 Electrification Dataset, World Bank (WB), uri: https://trackingsdg7.esmap.org/downloads, note: Data is downloaded from ESMAP website. Data is released when a new Tracking SDG7 report is released., publisher: World Bank (WB), date accessed: 2024-05-16, date published: 2023

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for rural electricity access.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Venezuela, RB, Uzbekistan, United States, while the lightest include Chad, Congo, Dem. Rep., South Sudan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the rural electricity access pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Rural electricity access may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Electricity access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Basic sanitation access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Basic drinking water access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Energy maps often separate access, consumption, and production mix.
Look for hydro, fossil-fuel, and low-access signals.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.
